passages.title: Computer games
passages.source_html:
Computer games


The early days of the video game business


A


It's not whether you win or lose, but how you play the game.In the age of computers, that statement takes on new meanings: video game cannot ever really be defeated because, no matter how high the score. It is always the human who tires first or makes the fatal error. But millions of people continue to play, because microelectronic technology has enabled game designers to conveniently and inexpensively transform plain screens into playfields of extraordinary capability. At the same time, a multi-billion dollar industry has grown from very humble beginnings in just a few decades.


B 


The technological roots of video game can be traced back to 1962, when an MIT (Massachusetts Institute of Technology) graduate student demonstrated Spacewar, a science-fiction fantasy game played on a mainframe computer and a large screen. That game immediately attracted a wide cult following among computer buffs. The next important step came in 1968, when a console was developed that could be used to play game on ordinary televisions. But it was not until the early 1970s that Bushnell, a young graduate student in engineering at the University of Utah, came to the point of adapting Spacewar from a large computer into coin-operated form. For use in video game arcades, using electronics was becoming economically feasible. Bushnell and his associates began working on such a machine in a converted bedroom workshop, but were unsuccessful. What they ultimately developed instead was a simple tennis-like game that they named Pong.


C 


Pong took the industry by storm and quickly became the first coin-operated video-game. And soon thereafter commercial Pong-style home video games also appeared. Yet despite early enthusiasm, consumer interest in this area proved less sustained than had been anticipated and, as prices started to drop and losses mounted, most of the early manufacturers withdrew from the field. Profits proved to be just as elusive at Bushnell's company, Atari, where a rapidly growing market presence in coin-operated machine and home video required greater injections of capital and more professional management than the company was able to provide. In 1976, the founders of Atari sold their share of the company for a sum that was only equivalent to their sales in that year.


D 


At that point, coin-operated video games seemed just another passing fad. But the introduction of Space Invaders-an arcade model produced by Japanese manufacture Taito-proved otherwise. With its vibrant graphics it was so different from the previous black and white games that Space Invaders immediately captured public interest. There soon followed a rush of popular video that employed the same or better hardware and even more imaginative software. Of these, Pac-Man (in 1980) was especially significant, because now females began to take an interest.


E 


By this time, the same software improvement and technological advances (faster microprocessors and larger memories) that permitted designers to produce spectacular audio and visual effects for coin-operated machines were also being applied to home video units. It was thus only a short while before the programmable consoles that had been unpopular for lack of software suddenly began to sell in large numbers: consumers had discovered that they could finally play a reasonable version of their favorite arcade games in the comfort of their own home. The impact on Atari was astounding. Unprofitable for the first three years, Atari had by the end of 1979, become a success. By either self-designing or licensing the most popular arcade concepts for cartridge format for use at home, the company had captured some 80 percent of the worldwide market for home video games.


F 


All of this, however, was too good to last. By late 1982, the publics fascination with arcade games had begun to low down, and fewer potential best-sellers were becoming available for conversion to cartridges that could be used on an Atari machine. At the same time, the market was flooded with illegal software of all types. It was thus not until the late 1980s that the unstructured nature of the industry, at least on the software side, had stabilized and become restructured in a manner similar, in many respects, to the book publishing business.


G 


Until 1986, when Japan-based Nintendo introduced a more technologically sophisticated and user-friendly game console, the hardware side was also in disarray. But with tight control of software development and marketing, Nintendo was able to revive and then capture up to 80 percent of a once-again booming market in which no significant competition appeared until the early 1990s. By that point, the annual operating profits of Nintendo had already grown to over $1 billion – an amount exceeding the 1991 profits of all the major Hollywood film studios combined. In 1999 sales of game hardware and software, led by Playstation, were equal in size (around $7 billion) to US domestic box-office revenues.


H 


With change the only constant, the game industry has moved on to become what it is today. However, no matter what the technology or the format, the essence of a successful game will always be the same: it is simple to understand and to play on an elementary level, but it is compulsive and maddeningly difficult – in fact, forever impossible-to master fully.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1 - 6


Complete the notes. Write ONE WORD ONLY in each gap.

1

A history of video games

1960s

• Spacewar was first played on a computer and special screen.

1970s

• Advances in technology led to cheaper  and the possibility of coin-operated video games.

正确答案： 1 electronics
Question 1

审题：题目问的是1970年代，由于技术进步使得某个东西变得便宜，从而促成了投币视频游戏的可能性。


定位关键词：cheaper，coin-operated video games


对应原文：B段“For use in video game arcades, using electronics was becoming economically feasible. ”


解题思路：B段介绍了视频游戏的发展历史，指出在 1970年代，技术进步导致了使用电子设备从经济的角度变得可行。


同义替换：cheaper  =economically feasible

2

• The first successful coin-operated video game was

正确答案： 2 Pong
Question 2

审题：题目问的是第一个成功的投币游戏是什么。


定位关键词：first，coin-operated video game


对应原文：C段“Pong took the industry by storm and quickly became the first coin-operated video-game. ” 


解题思路：本段介绍了视频游戏的发展历史，指出 Pong 是第一个成功的投币式视频游戏。


同义替换：successful = took the industry by storm 

3

•  was bought from its original owners.

正确答案： 3 Atari
Question 3

审题：题目问的是哪家公司被原始拥有者卖掉了。


定位关键词：bought，original owners


对应原文：C段“Yet despite early enthusiasm, consumer interest in this area proved less sustained than had been anticipated and, as prices started to drop and losses mounted, most of the early manufacturers withdrew from the field. Profits proved to be just as elusive at Bushnell's company, Atari, where a rapidly growing market presence in coin-operated machine and home video required greater injections of capital and more professional management than the company was able to provide. In 1976, the founders of Atari sold their share of the company for a sum that was only equivalent to their sales in that year.”


解题思路：本文介绍了 Atari 公司的历史，指出其创始人于 1976 年将其股份出售。


同义替换：original owners = founders

4

• Space Invaders was successful because of its colourful

正确答案： 4 graphics
Question 4

审题：题目问的是Space Invaders因为什么特色成功。


定位关键词：Space Invaders，colorful


对应原文：D段“With its vibrant graphics it was so different from the previous black and white games that Space Invaders immediately captured public interest.”


解题思路：本段介绍了《太空侵略者》游戏的成功，指出其原因之一是其鲜艳的图像vibrant graphics，这与之前的黑白游戏截然不同。


同义替换：colourful = vibrant graphics

5

1980s

• Pac-Man was the first game to attract

正确答案： 5 females
Question 5

审题：题目问的是哪个群体第一次被Pac-Man吸引。


定位关键词：Pac-Man，attract


对应原文：D段“Of these, Pac-Man (in 1980) was especially significant, because now females began to take an interest.”


解题思路：本文介绍了《吃豆人》游戏的影响，指出它首次吸引了女性的兴趣。


同义替换：attract = take an interest.

6

1990s

• At first one company dominated the market.

• By the end of the decade  had become the biggest selling home entertainment product.

正确答案： 6 Playstation
Question 6

审题：题目问的是在90年代末，哪款产品成为了最畅销的家庭娱乐产品。


定位关键词：“By the end of the decade”和the biggest selling home entertainment product


对应原文：G段“ In 1999 sales of game hardware and software, led by Playstation, were equal in size (around $7 billion) to US domestic box-office revenues.”


解题思路：本文最后总结了视频游戏的发展历史，指出到 1990 年代末，视频游戏已成为最畅销的家用娱乐产品。

Questions 7 - 13


Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

7

Spacewar was unpopular at first.

正确答案： B. FALSE

审题：题目问的是《太空战争》最初是否不受欢迎。


定位关键信息：关键词“Spacewar” 和 “unpopular”。


对应原文：B段,The technological roots of video game can be traced back to 1962, when an MIT (Massachusetts Institute of Technology) graduate student demonstrated Spacewar, a science-fiction fantasy game played on a mainframe computer and a large screen. That game immediately attracted a wide cult following among computer buffs.


解题思路：《太空战争》立即吸引了大量粉丝。


8

Bushnell and his team failed to create a coin-operated version of Spacewar.

正确答案： A. TRUE

审题：题目问的是布什内尔和他的团队是否未能制作出投币版本的《太空战争》。


定位关键信息：Bushnell, coin-operated version


对应原文：But it was not until the early 1970s that Bushnell, a young graduate student in engineering at the University of Utah, came to the point of adapting Spacewar from a large computer into coin-operated form. For use in video game arcades, using electronics was becoming economically feasible. Bushnell and his associates began working on such a machine in a converted bedroom workshop, but were unsuccessful. 


解题思路：原文指出，20 世纪 70 年代初，当时还是犹他大学工程专业年轻研究生的布什内尔才着手将《太空大战》从大型计算机改编成投币式游戏。由于电子技术的发展，将其用于街机游戏在经济上变得可行。布什内尔和他的同事们在一个改装过的卧室工作室里开始研制这样的机器，但没有成功。


同义替换：failed to create=were unsuccessful

9

From the beginning the home video game market has been commercially successful.

正确答案： B. FALSE

审题：题目问的是家用视频游戏市场从一开始是否就取得了商业成功。


定位关键信息：关键词“home video game market” 和 “commercially successful”。


对应原文：C段，Yet despite early enthusiasm, consumer interest in this area proved less sustained than had been anticipated and, as prices started to drop and losses mounted, most of the early manufacturers withdrew from the field. 


解题思路：原文说明，尽管起初热情高涨，但消费者在这一领域的兴趣却不如预期那样持久。随着价格开始下降，亏损不断增加，大多数早期制造商纷纷退出了这一领域。这表明早期家用视频游戏市场并未取得成功。

10

Atari was successful for the first time in 1979.

正确答案： A. TRUE

审题：题目问的是Atari公司是否在1979年首次取得成功。


定位关键信息：关键词“Atari” 和 “successful in 1979”。


对应原文：E段，The impact on Atari was astounding. Unprofitable for the first three years, Atari had by the end of 1979, become a success.  


解题思路：Atari在1979年取得了首次成功。


同义替换：successful=become a success    

11

Video arcade game usage continued strongly in the 1980s.

正确答案： B. FALSE

审题：题目问的是街机游戏在80年代是否持续流行。


定位关键信息：关键词“video arcade game usage” 和 “continued strongly in the 1980s”。


对应原文：F段，By late 1982, the publics fascination with arcade games had begun to low down, and fewer potential best-sellers were becoming available for conversion to cartridges that could be used on an Atari machine.   


解题思路：街机游戏的热度在80年代减弱。


12

The time taken to produce a video game can be compared to producing a book.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

审题：题目问的是制作视频游戏的时间是否可与制作书籍相比较。


定位关键信息：关键词“time taken” 和 “compared to producing a book”。


对应原文：F段，At the same time, the market was flooded with illegal software of all types. It was thus not until the late 1980s that the unstructured nature of the industry, at least on the software side, had stabilized and become restructured in a manner similar, in many respects, to the book publishing business.


解题思路：原文提到视频游戏开发和图书出版有相似性，但未提到具体的时间。


同义替换： time taken=restructured in a manner similar    

13

The qualities needed for a video game to become successful have been researched thoroughly.

正确答案： A. TRUE

审题：题目问的是成功视频游戏的要素是否已被彻底研究。


定位关键信息：关键词“qualities needed” 和 “researched thoroughly”。


对应原文：H段，However, no matter what the technology or the format, the essence of a successful game will always be the same: it is simple to understand and to play on an elementary level, but it is compulsive and maddeningly difficult – in fact, forever impossible-to master fully.


解题思路：原文提到无论技术和形式如何发展，游戏成功的要素是相同的，对应题目中游戏的要素是否已被彻底研究。


同义替换：qualities =essence
